ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling food product class; no specific Korean mandu or kimchi-pork dumpling subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers dumplings generically. No specific Korean mandu or kimchi-pork variant code exists in FoodEx2; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for Korean pork and kimchi dumplings. SNOMED CT does not have sufficient granularity for this food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of pork and kimchi dumplings is 5 pieces, weighing approximately 175 g, as served at Korean restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Mandu (Korean dumplings, pork and vegetable filling, steamed/pan-fried) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and adapted Korean food composition data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Korean Food Composition Database (NIAS, Rural Development Administration, Korea)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for pork-and-kimchi mandu exists in a single approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entries for pork and vegetable dumplings (FDC ID 168927, 'Dumplings, meat filled') and cross-referenced with Korean Food Composition Database (NIAS RDA Korea) data for mandu. Kimchi contribution to sodium and fibre is factored in. Per-100g values represent a blended estimate for a typical pork-and-kimchi mandu product.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of wheat-based dumplings and filled pasta products — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for filled pasta and steamed wheat dumplings
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Korean pork-and-kimchi mandu in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 48 is estimated by analogy with steamed wheat-flour dumplings and filled pasta products (GI range 39–55, low category) reported in the University of Sydney GI Database. The wheat-flour wrapper dominates glycemic response; pork and kimchi filling moderates it. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 39.4) / 100 ≈ 18.9. Carbohydrate per serving = 22.5 g/100g × 175 g = 39.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from kimchi, soy sauce, and seasoned pork filling
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit pork and kimchi dumpling intake to occasional consumption, as a 5-piece serving provides approximately 840 mg sodium (35% of the 2,400 mg daily limit), primarily from kimchi fermentation brine and soy-based seasoning.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from wheat-flour wrapper; protein and fat content attenuate postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can consume pork and kimchi dumplings in controlled portions, as the low GI (approximately 48) and protein-fat content of the filling moderate postprandial blood glucose; however, the glycemic load of approximately 18.9 per 5-piece serving warrants portion awareness and pairing with non-starchy vegetables.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat and high sodium; pork filling contributes cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful that a 5-piece serving of pork and kimchi dumplings contributes approximately 4.2 g saturated fat and 840 mg sodium, both of which may adversely affect lipid profiles and blood pressure when consumed frequently; steamed preparation is preferable to pan-fried to reduce total fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pork and kimchi dumplings (Korean: 돼지고기 김치 만두, romanized: Dwaeji-gogi Kimchi Mandu) are a traditional Korean stuffed dumpling dish consisting of a thin wheat-flour wrapper encasing a savoury filling of seasoned ground pork and fermented kimchi, typically also containing garlic, ginger, green onion, and sesame oil. The dumplings may be steamed, pan-fried (yielding a crispy bottom), or boiled, each method producing a distinct texture. Steamed mandu are soft and tender throughout, while pan-fried versions (gunmandu) develop a golden, slightly crispy exterior. The flavour profile is umami-rich, tangy from the kimchi fermentation, and mildly spicy, with aromatic notes from sesame and garlic. In Singapore, pork and kimchi dumplings are served at Korean restaurants as an appetiser or side dish, typically in portions of 5 pieces. Nutritionally, a 5-piece serving (approximately 175 g) provides around 341 kcal, 16.6 g protein, 13.7 g fat, and 39.4 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is notably high at approximately 840 mg per serving due to kimchi and soy-based seasoning, making this a food of concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 48 (low), making it a moderate glycemic-load food suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes in controlled portions. Kimchi contributes dietary fibre, vitamin C, and beneficial lactic acid bacteria from fermentation, adding nutritional value beyond the macronutrient profile.
common_user_queries: Are pork and kimchi dumplings healthy?
How many calories in Korean dumplings?
What is the GI of Korean dumplings?
How many calories in 5 pieces of mandu?
Is mandu suitable for high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact pork-and-kimchi mandu entry was found in a single approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central pork-and-vegetable dumpling data and cross-referenced with Korean Food Composition Database (NIAS RDA Korea) mandu entries. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for wheat-flour dumplings and filled pasta. Confidence is medium rather than low because the food type is well-characterised in approved databases and the adaptation is methodologically sound.
notes: 1. Food name corrected from input typo 'Dumpligs' to canonical 'Dumplings'. 2. Nutrient values are adapted estimates; no single approved source provides an exact pork-and-kimchi mandu entry. Values cross-referenced between USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 168927, meat-filled dumplings) and Korean Food Composition Database (NIAS RDA Korea). 3. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary reference sources for this food type. 4. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary reference sources for this food type. 5. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary reference sources for this food type. 6. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for steamed wheat-flour dumplings and filled pasta (GI range 39–55); no direct GI measurement for pork-and-kimchi mandu is available. 7. Serving size of 175 g (5 pieces) is estimated based on typical restaurant portion weights for Korean mandu (approximately 35 g per piece). 8. Halal status is not confirmed; pork is a primary ingredient, making this dish non-halal. 9. The input food_id GD001015 and restaurant attribution (Ajumma's - Funan, field survey data) are preserved from the seed record for traceability.
